Active Work Architecture
─[ Skill and Agent Ownership ]─ ┐

Skill and Agent Ownership

Implement the decisions from the skill-agent-ownership brainstorm. Formalizes the lore-* prefix as the ownership boundary, adds per-platform model configuration, and updates all affected documentation across three repos.

Goal

Clear separation between framework-owned and operator-owned skills/agents. Operators never worry about Lore overwriting their content. Per-platform model preferences work across Claude Code, OpenCode, and Cursor.

Scope

Batch 1: Lore Source — Frontmatter and Schema Cleanup

Remove dead metadata and establish the new frontmatter schema.

  • Remove scope: internal from all 14 framework skill frontmatter files
  • Rename documentation-agent.md → lore-documentation-agent.md
  • Replace model: sonnet with per-platform fields (claude-model, opencode-model, cursor-model) on agent
  • Add subagentDefaults to .lore-config
  • Regenerate platform copies and registries

Batch 2: Lore Source — Script Changes

Update scripts to respect the lore-* ownership boundary.

  • sync-framework.sh — filter skill/agent sync loop to lore-* only
  • generate-agents.sh — filter to lore-* skill domains only
  • sync-platform-skills.sh — resolve model cascade (agent frontmatter → subagentDefaults → omit), write platform-native model field
  • lib/config.js — read subagentDefaults from .lore-config
  • validate-consistency.sh — tiered validation (basic for all skills, strict for lore-*)

Batch 3: Lore Source — Templates and Instructions

Update the canonical instructions and skill/agent creation flows.

  • .lore/instructions.md — add ownership rules, agent naming convention, model field guidance
  • .lore/skills/lore-create-skill/SKILL.md — drop scope from template, add naming/ownership guidance
  • .lore/skills/lore-create-agent/SKILL.md — per-platform model fields in template, lore- prefix for framework agents, subagentDefaults reference
  • README.md — ownership convention clarity
  • CONTRIBUTING.md — framework/operator distinction

Batch 4: Lore Docs Site

Update public documentation to reflect new conventions.

  • guides/configuration.md — add subagentDefaults section, per-platform model docs
  • how-it-works.md — ownership convention, model configuration section
  • guides/platform-support.md — sync boundaries section
  • examples/skill.md — remove scope from example, add naming convention note
  • getting-started.md — ownership convention in directory structure
  • guides/commands.md — /lore-update boundary docs
  • index.md — clarify framework vs operator in component table

Batch 5: Sync and Validate

  • Run full test suite in lore source (npm test — 156/156 pass)
  • Commit and push lore source
  • Commit and push lore-docs
  • Sync framework into lorehq
  • bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh in lorehq
  • Commit and push lorehq

Deliverables

  • All 14 skills have no scope field
  • Agent renamed to lore-documentation-agent with per-platform model fields
  • sync-framework.sh only overwrites lore-* skills/agents
  • generate-agents.sh only processes lore-* skill domains
  • sync-platform-skills.sh resolves model cascade and writes platform-native values
  • .lore-config supports subagentDefaults with per-platform model keys
  • All 7 lore-docs pages updated
  • All 3 repos committed and passing validation

Success Criteria

  • Operator-created skill (non-lore-*) survives /lore-update without being overwritten
  • Agent with per-platform model fields generates correct platform-native copies
  • subagentDefaults in .lore-config are inherited by agents that omit model fields
  • npm test passes with no regressions
  • validate-consistency.sh passes in both lore source and lorehq

Test Plan

# Lore source
npm test
npm run lint
bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh
bash scripts/check-version-sync.sh

# lorehq (after sync)
bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh
bash scripts/generate-nav.sh

Manual verification:

  • Create a test skill without lore- prefix, run sync-framework.sh, confirm it survives
  • Set subagentDefaults in .lore-config, create agent without model fields, confirm platform copy gets the default
  • Set per-platform model on agent, confirm it overrides the instance default
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
